Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Discover the ExpenseBot project and list all its Linear issues.
The agent uses linear_list_projects (query: "expense" or similar) to find the project "Expense Auto-Categorization Engine" (proj_expense_autocategorizer), then uses linear_list_issues with project filter to retrieve all issues under the project. Discovers 20 issues spanning multiple states: 4 epics (ENG-201 through ENG-204, all state_backlog), implementation tickets (ENG-205 through ENG-209, all state_todo), pilot monitoring (ENG-211, state_in_progress), edge case coverage (ENG-212, state_in_progress), dashboard extras (ENG-213, state_backlog), P1 config fixes (ENG-214 and ENG-215, state_in_progress), urgent audit (ENG-216, state_in_progress), pipeline hardening (ENG-217, state_in_progress), Anh's type validation (ENG-218, state_in_progress), launch readiness checklist (ENG-219, state_todo), ML v2 backlog (ENG-210, state_backlog), and duplicate detection (ENG-220, state_todo). Many tickets show states (state_todo, state_backlog) that may not reflect actual completion based on team discussions.
→ Read/lookup action. linear_list_projects and linear_list_issues are non-default tools.
OE 2: Read comments on key Linear issues to determine actual work status.
The agent uses linear_list_comments on multiple issues to discover completion evidence and incident context:
- issue_admin_dashboard (ENG-203): Anh's comment (comment_anh_dashboard_done, Feb 27) confirms dashboard is operational in staging with all core features delivered: expense review queue, detail view, manual override, audit trail, and rule configuration UI.
- issue_policy_config (ENG-202): Lena's comment (comment_lena_engine_staging, Feb 27) explicitly states "Marking this epic as complete", all three sub-components (ENG-205 CategoryMatcher, ENG-206 PolicyValidator, ENG-207 DecisionEngine) are developed and operational end-to-end in staging.
- issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217): Dmitri's comment (comment_dmitri_audit_results, Mar 20) reports full audit results: 47 expenses reviewed, 39 correct, 5 false rejections (all Vectral), 3 false approvals (all Mosaic), $885 total financial exposure.
- issue_p1_vectral_config (ENG-214): Lena's comment (comment_lena_vectral_fix_plan) confirms immediate config corrections and removal of silent fallback in ingestion pipeline.
- issue_p1_mosaic_malformed (ENG-215): Lena's comment (comment_lena_deploy_verified, Mar 18) confirms config corrections deployed and verified in production, tested Danielle Osei $475 chair (correctly approved), Kevin Tran $185 internet (correctly approved with edge case noted), Andre Washington $1,200 desk (correctly denied), Andre coworking (correctly denied). States "Closing ENG-214. Now starting on pipeline hardening (ENG-217)."
- issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216): Dmitri's comment (comment_dmitri_duplicate_discovery, Mar 15) details three flagged stipend transactions, Kevin Tran (Vectral) and Andre Washington (Mosaic) with byte-for-byte identical $89 receipt SHA-256 hashes (a3f7c8d1e9b2...f0a2b4), and Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop) with matching PDF template metadata (TC-RECEIPT-STD-041). Total potential exposure $223 ($89 already paid, $134 pending).
- issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220): Dmitri's comment (comment_dmitri_duplicate_ticket_context, Mar 15) references audit findings, Airtable records (rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand), QuickBooks holds by Marcus (STIP-2026-0094, STIP-2026-0097), and AM verification requests sent to Vectral (Mina → Rachel Whitfield), Mosaic (Emeka → Tamara Byrd), and BrightLoop (Emeka → Tessa Moreno).
- issue_pilot_monitoring (ENG-211): Multiple comments including Samira's initial incident report (comment_false_rejection_incident), Mina's two-incident report (comment_mina_two_incidents: Danielle Osei $475 chair and Kevin Tran $185 internet), Emeka's false approvals report (comment_emeka_false_approvals: Andre Washington $1,200 desk and $350 coworking), Lena's RCA (comment_monitoring_rca_update: both incidents linked to policy ingestion pipeline ENG-209), Marcus's finance request (comment_marcus_audit_request), David's client impact assessment (comment_david_client_impact), Elena's CEO directive (comment_elena_directive: pause auto-approve/deny, MoveOps covers $1,550, full audit by Wed 3/19), and Samira's deployment confirmation (comment_samira_pause_confirmed: auto-decisions disabled, remediation timeline with ENG-216/217/218/212/219).
- issue_category_matcher (ENG-205): Samira's comment (comment_policy_ingestion_unblocked) confirms policy data received from Chloe with specific Vectral and Mosaic configurations; Lena's comment (comment_lena_exclusion_handling) details exclusion handling revisions.
- issue_edge_cases_backlog (ENG-212): Dmitri's comment (comment_dmitri_validation_tests, Mar 20) confirms 6 schema validation tests implemented, all pass with corrected configs, all fail with original production configs confirming they would have caught both root causes pre-deployment.
→ Read/lookup action. linear_list_comments is a non-default tool.
OE 3: Search Slack #engineering (C003) for ExpenseBot project discussions.
The agent uses conversations_search_messages (query: "expense categorizer" or "auto-categorizer" or "dashboard" or similar) and/or conversations_history (channel_id: C003) to find team discussions. Key discoveries:
- Anh's March 5 progress update confirming the admin dashboard (ENG-203) and rule configuration UI (ENG-208) are operational in staging with all core features delivered.
- Lena's March 5 confirmation that CategoryMatcher (ENG-205), PolicyValidator (ENG-206), DecisionEngine (ENG-207), and ingestion pipeline (ENG-209) are all completed and operational in staging. Also confirms "ENG-201 complete."
- Samira's March 5 project summary noting ENG-201–203 done, ENG-204 in progress.
- Mina's March 16 cross-post about Vectral false rejections: Danielle Osei $475 chair denied (system applied $400 limit instead of correct $600), Kevin Tran $185 internet setup.
- Emeka's March 16 post about Mosaic false approvals: Andre Washington $1,200 standing desk ($800 cap) and $350/month coworking (excluded category), ≥$1,550 exposure.
- Lena's RCA post identifying root causes in the policy ingestion pipeline (ENG-209): Vectral, silent parse error fallback applied default template limits; Mosaic, malformed JSON types caused exclusion check bypass and string comparison bug.
- Samira's April 4 remediation strategy: auto-approve/deny disabled for both pilots, audit plan, remediation timeline (ENG-216 audit by Wed, ENG-217 pipeline by Thu, ENG-218/212 by ~3/27, re-enable ~3/30).
- Lena's April 4 deployment confirmation: auto_decisions_enabled: false, corrected Vectral limits, Mosaic excluded_categories changed from string to array.
- Dmitri's audit findings post: 39 correct, 5 false rejections, 3 false approvals, $885 total exposure.
- Dmitri's separate post about duplicate receipt anomalies: SHA-256 hash matches between Kevin Tran (Vectral) and Andre Washington (Mosaic) for $89 keyboards, plus Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop) $45 monitor stand with matching PDF template.
- Dmitri's ENG-220 filing announcement for duplicate detection system with receipt hash fingerprinting.
These discussions confirm that multiple tickets (ENG-205, 206, 207, 208, 209) were discussed as "done" in Slack but their Linear states were never updated, confirming the persona's suspicion.
→ Read/lookup action. conversations_search_messages and conversations_history are default tools.
OE 4: Read Slack thread replies on key engineering discussions.
The agent uses conversations_replies on key threads in C003 to discover detailed sub-discussions and confirmations buried in threads. Thread replies contain Samira's architecture decisions and task assignments, Lena's deployment confirmations and config fix specifics (corrected limits, string-to-array fix for excluded_categories), Dmitri's schema validation test details (6 tests implemented: test_config_field_types, test_required_fields, test_config_matches_source, test_exclusion_entries_valid, test_limit_values_positive, test_no_silent_fallback), Anh's dashboard validation proposals, and team discussion about the remediation timeline and launch readiness gate requirements.
→ Read/lookup action. conversations_replies is a default tool.
OE 5: Cross-reference and update stale Linear tickets, close completed ones.
The agent uses linear_update_issue to change state to "done" (or equivalent) for tickets where Slack/comments confirm completion but Linear still shows open:
- issue_category_matcher (ENG-205): Lena confirmed complete (Mar 5 Slack: "✅ CategoryMatcher, completed"; Feb 27 Linear comment on ENG-202 lists it as done; RCA confirms core engine logic is functioning correctly), state is state_todo → close.
- issue_policy_validator (ENG-206): Lena confirmed complete (Mar 5 Slack: "✅ PolicyValidator, completed"; Feb 27 Linear comment on ENG-202; same RCA clearance), state is state_todo → close.
- issue_decision_engine (ENG-207): Lena confirmed complete (Mar 5 Slack: "✅ DecisionEngine, completed"; Feb 27 Linear comment on ENG-202; same RCA clearance), state is state_todo → close.
- issue_policy_ingestion (ENG-209): Lena marked complete in Slack (Mar 5: "✅ Policy ingestion (ENG-209), completed"), but the RCA later attributed the pilot incident directly to this pipeline ("Both client incidents are linked to the policy ingestion pipeline (ENG-209)"), and ENG-217 is actively hardening it. A cautious agent may reasonably skip this ticket. State is state_todo.
- issue_rule_config_ui (ENG-208): Anh confirmed delivered as part of dashboard update (March 5 Slack + Feb 27 Linear comment listing "Rule configuration UI" among delivered features), but no one explicitly says "ENG-208 is done", completion is inferred from the feature being listed among delivered dashboard capabilities. State is state_todo.
Additional tickets where the agent may determine completion and close: issue_policy_config (ENG-202, Lena's comment explicitly says "Marking this epic as complete," state is state_backlog), issue_admin_dashboard (ENG-203, confirmed done by Anh's comment, state is state_backlog), and potentially ENG-214/215 (config fixes confirmed deployed by Lena, Lena's comment says "Closing ENG-214"). However, the persona is a junior engineer (Anh Nguyen) who should be parsimonious, closing epics is typically the project lead's prerogative, and P1 incident tickets (ENG-214/215) should not be closed by a junior team member while remediation is still ongoing. The rubric threshold of "at least 4" reflects this expectation of appropriate caution: the undeniable core set is ENG-205, 206, 207 (3 tickets with explicit completion confirmation and RCA clearance), with ENG-208 and ENG-209 as reasonable additional candidates depending on the agent's judgment.
→ Write action → goes into Outcome rubrics only.
OE 6: Add a progress comment on Anh's type validation ticket.
The agent uses linear_create_comment on issue_dashboard_type_validation (ENG-218) with body indicating: finished numeric validation for max_amount fields, now working on array serialization fix for excluded_categories, target completion Friday.
→ Write action → goes into Outcome rubrics only.
OE 7: Query Airtable for stipend records to verify data status.
The agent uses airtable_list_bases to discover the MoveOps Operations base (appMoveOpsOps001), then airtable_list_tables to find the Stipend Transactions table (tblStipends00001), then airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records to retrieve stipend records. Key discoveries:
- rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard: Kevin Tran (Vectral), $89 ergonomic keyboard from TechComfort Supply, Approval Status = "Approved", Audit Flag = "Duplicate Hash", auto-approved and paid out on 2026-03-08 before the ExpenseBot pause. Receipt hash identical to Andre Washington's submission.
- rec_stipend_andre_keyboard: Andre Washington (Mosaic), $89 ergonomic keyboard from TechComfort Supply, Approval Status = "Pending Approval", Audit Flag = "Duplicate Hash", held by finance. Same SHA-256 hash as Kevin Tran's submission (byte-for-byte identical PDF).
- rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand: Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop), $45 monitor stand from TechComfort Supply, Approval Status = "Pending Approval", Audit Flag = "PDF Template Match", held by finance. Same PDF /Creator metadata and /Template ID (TC-RECEIPT-STD-041) as the duplicate pair.
The agent verifies that the held records (Andre Washington, Jordan Ekwueme) correctly show "Pending Approval" reflecting the admin holds, while Kevin Tran's record shows "Approved" despite being flagged, a data quality concern for relaunch since it was paid out before the pause.
The agent may also discover the broader ExpenseBot error records in the same table: false rejections still pending manual correction (Maria Santos $550 standing desk, James Okafor $82 keyboard tray, Lisa Park $130 mesh WiFi, all Vectral) and false approvals absorbed by MoveOps (Andre Washington $1,200 desk, Andre Washington $350 coworking, Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking passes, all Mosaic).
→ Read/lookup action. airtable_list_bases, airtable_list_tables, airtable_list_records, airtable_search_records are non-default tools.
OE 8: Verify QuickBooks bills to confirm stipend payment statuses and admin holds.
The agent uses quickbooks_search_bills (or quickbooks_get_bill with specific IDs) to cross-reference the financial records for the three flagged stipend transactions. Key discoveries:
- STIP-2026-0089 (bill_stipend_tran_keyboard): Kevin Tran (Vectral), $89, Balance = $0, fully paid/disbursed on 2026-03-08. Description confirms auto-approved by ExpenseBot before the pause. Cannot be clawed back without Vectral HR verification.
- STIP-2026-0094 (bill_stipend_washington_keyboard): Andre Washington (Mosaic), $89, Balance = $89, pending approval, NOT disbursed. A separate hold record (bill_stipend_washington_keyboard_hold, SyncToken "1") confirms admin hold placed by Marcus Thorne on 2026-03-15 due to duplicate receipt hash matching Kevin Tran's submission.
- STIP-2026-0097 (bill_stipend_ekwueme_monitor): Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop), $45, Balance = $45, pending approval, NOT disbursed. A separate hold record (bill_stipend_ekwueme_monitor_hold, SyncToken "1") confirms admin hold placed by Marcus Thorne on 2026-03-15 due to PDF template metadata matching duplicate cluster TC-RECEIPT-STD-041.
This cross-references with and confirms the Airtable approval statuses, providing independent financial verification that the holds are in place and the approval statuses are correct before surfacing them in the dashboard.
→ Read/lookup action. quickbooks_search_bills and quickbooks_get_bill are non-default tools.
OE 9: Search Slack #finance (C005) for expense-related financial exposure discussions.
The agent uses conversations_search_messages (query: "expense" or "stipend" or "hold" or similar) and/or conversations_history (channel_id: C005) to discover financial team discussions about the ExpenseBot impact. Key discoveries:
- Marcus's post about the pilot bug: ≥$1,550 auto-approved out-of-policy for Mosaic ($1,200 desk over $800 cap, $350/month coworking not covered), requesting halt of auto-approvals, review of all auto-processed expenses for both pilots, and no expansion until pipeline verified.
- Marcus's stipend hold post: admin holds placed on STIP-2026-0094 (Andre Washington, Mosaic, $89 duplicate receipt hash) and STIP-2026-0097 (Jordan Ekwueme, BrightLoop, $45 matched PDF template). Kevin Tran's STIP-2026-0089 ($89) already paid out 2026-03-08. On hold: $134; if all three fraudulent: $223 total.
- Hana's confirmation of investigation flags on STIP-2026-0089, 0094, and 0097; will scan ledger for other TechComfort Supply stipends.
- Marcus's blanket hold on pending stipend claims until fraud review complete.
→ Read/lookup action. conversations_search_messages and conversations_history are default tools.
OE 10: Search emails for pilot client complaints and internal escalations about client impact.
The agent uses search_emails (query: "expense rejected" or "ergonomic chair" or "Vectral" or "Danielle" or similar) to find the complaint chains and internal escalation emails. Key discoveries:
- email_rachel_complaint: Rachel Whitfield (rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com), VP of People at Vectral Systems, emailed Mina (mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) complaining that Danielle Osei's $475 ergonomic office chair was incorrectly rejected with the reason "exceeds category limit", the actual Vectral ergonomic limit is $600, not the $400 the system applied. Rachel states "We even raised that limit last quarter."
- email_mina_to_rachel_apology: Mina apologized, confirmed the system error ("a new automated expense review tool... applied an incorrect limit"), and manually approved the expense for processing within 1-2 business days.
- email_mina_to_samira_escalation: Mina escalated to Samira (CC: Chloe, David) with subject "URGENT" reporting two false rejections, Danielle Osei's $475 chair (system applied $400 instead of $600 ergonomic limit) and Kevin Tran's $185 internet setup fee (system failed to differentiate setup fees from the $150 monthly cap). Mina reports Rachel's tone has become "noticeably more frosty."
- email_emeka_false_approvals: Emeka reported to Samira and Marcus (CC: Chloe, David) that the auto-categorizer approved out-of-policy expenses for Mosaic, Andre Washington's $1,200 standing desk (Mosaic cap $800, $400 over, system failed to enforce limit) and $350/month coworking membership (explicitly excluded from coverage, system didn't flag exclusion). Total initial exposure ≥$1,550. Emeka notes Mosaic HR hasn't noticed yet, quarterly reconciliation in ~6 weeks will reveal it.
- email_david_ce_escalation: David Chen emailed Elena and Samira (CC: Marcus, Chloe, Mina, Emeka) assessing client relationship impact, Vectral trust eroding (Rachel reassessing reliability of MoveOps, tone change, another incident would trigger internal escalation), Mosaic facing ticking clock before quarterly reconciliation. Both AMs losing confidence in the tool and manually reviewing every decision.
- email_elena_directive: Elena's pause directive, auto-approve/deny paused immediately, full audit by Wednesday 3/19, MoveOps covers the $1,550, Mina to call Rachel, Emeka to proactively inform Mosaic HR.
The Vectral complaint was resolved (manual override + config fix + apology), but the chain reveals the severity of the configuration bug and the client trust impact.
→ Read/lookup action. search_emails is a default tool.
OE 11: Look up contact details for Samira Tariq (and Anh Nguyen for sender).
The agent uses contacts_search_contacts (query: "Samira") to find Samira Tariq's email address: samira.tariq@moveops.com. May also look up Anh's own email: anh.nguyen@moveops.com. These lookups are needed to correctly populate the sender and recipient fields for the relaunch readiness email.
→ Read/lookup action. contacts_search_contacts is a default tool.
OE 12: Post a comprehensive project status update in Slack #engineering.
The agent uses conversations_add_message with channel_id C003 (or discovers C003 via channels_list). The payload includes a comprehensive relaunch readiness summary covering:
- Ticket audit results: which tickets were confirmed done and closed (ENG-205, 206, 207, and potentially 208, 209), which are still in progress (ENG-212 edge case tests, Dmitri, ENG-218 type validation, Anh targeting Friday, ENG-211 pilot monitoring, Samira, ENG-217 pipeline hardening, Lena), and which remain in backlog or todo (ENG-210 ML v2, ENG-213 bulk export, ENG-219 launch readiness checklist, ENG-220 duplicate detection).
- Data integrity: 3 flagged stipend records from Dmitri's duplicate receipt audit (all TechComfort Supply). 2 held records (Andre Washington $89, Jordan Ekwueme $45) correctly reflecting admin holds in both Airtable ("Pending Approval") and QuickBooks (admin hold by Marcus, balances unpaid). 1 flagged record (Kevin Tran $89) still showing "Approved" in Airtable despite duplicate hash flag, paid out 2026-03-08 before the pause.
- Client impact summary: Vectral, Rachel Whitfield's complaint about Danielle Osei's $475 chair resolved (manually approved, config fixed, apology sent). 5 total Vectral false rejections found by audit, 2 corrected (Danielle, Kevin), 3 still pending manual intervention (Maria Santos $550, James Okafor $82, Lisa Park $130). Mosaic, 3 false approvals totaling $885 financial exposure ($400 desk overage + $350 coworking + $135 coworking passes), MoveOps absorbing costs per Elena's directive.
- Financial exposure: $885 from false approvals (Mosaic). $223 potential from duplicate receipt cluster ($89 Kevin Tran already paid, $134 on hold). 3 Vectral false rejections still pending manual correction.
- Remaining blockers for relaunch: type validation completion (ENG-218), pipeline hardening completion (ENG-217), edge case tests (ENG-212), launch readiness checklist sign-off (ENG-219), duplicate detection system implementation (ENG-220), Kevin Tran stipend record status resolution, manual correction of 3 remaining Vectral false rejections.
→ Write action → goes into Outcome rubrics only.
OE 13: Send Samira an email summarizing relaunch readiness.
The agent uses send_email with sender anh.nguyen@moveops.com, recipients [samira.tariq@moveops.com], and content covering:
- What's complete: core engine tickets (ENG-205 CategoryMatcher, ENG-206 PolicyValidator, ENG-207 DecisionEngine) all confirmed done and now closed in Linear. Dashboard (ENG-203) and rule config UI (ENG-208) operational in staging. Config fixes deployed and verified in production (ENG-214 Vectral, ENG-215 Mosaic). Full audit completed (ENG-216: 47 expenses reviewed, 39 correct, 83% accuracy rate, $885 financial exposure identified). Auto-decisions disabled, human review in place.
- What's still open: type validation (ENG-218, Anh, targeting Friday), edge case tests (ENG-212, Dmitri, 6 schema validation tests done, categorization edge cases remaining), pipeline hardening (ENG-217, Lena), launch readiness checklist (ENG-219, Samira), duplicate detection system (ENG-220, Dmitri, hash dedup layer ready to implement, PDF metadata parser planned).
- Client impact: Vectral, Rachel Whitfield's complaint resolved (Danielle Osei's $475 chair manually approved, config fixed, apology sent). 5 total false rejections found, 2 already corrected by Mina, 3 pending manual intervention (Maria Santos $550 standing desk, James Okafor $82 keyboard tray, Lisa Park $130 mesh WiFi). Mosaic, 3 false approvals totaling $885 exposure (Andre Washington $1,200 desk over $800 limit, Andre $350 coworking excluded, Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking passes excluded). MoveOps absorbing all costs per Elena's directive. Mosaic HR has not yet noticed, quarterly reconciliation in ~5-6 weeks will reveal it. Emeka instructed to proactively inform Mosaic HR.
- Data concerns: 3 flagged stipend records from Dmitri's duplicate receipt audit (all TechComfort Supply vendor). 2 (Andre Washington $89, Jordan Ekwueme $45) on admin hold with "Pending Approval" status, confirmed in both Airtable and QuickBooks. 1 (Kevin Tran $89) paid out before pause, shows "Approved" in Airtable despite duplicate hash flag, represents a data quality concern for relaunch. Total potential fraud exposure: $223.
- Recommendation: before relaunch, complete type validation (ENG-218), finalize edge case tests (ENG-212), complete pipeline hardening (ENG-217), resolve Kevin Tran stipend record status, manually correct 3 remaining Vectral false rejections, implement duplicate detection (ENG-220), and obtain sign-off on launch readiness checklist (ENG-219).
→ Write action → goes into Outcome rubrics only.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Discover the ExpenseBot project and list all its Linear issues.
The agent uses linear_list_projects (query: "expense" or similar) to find the project "Expense Auto-Categorization Engine" (proj_expense_autocategorizer), then uses linear_list_issues with project filter to retrieve all issues under the project. Discovers 20 issues spanning multiple states: 4 epics (ENG-201 through ENG-204, all state_backlog), implementation tickets (ENG-205 through ENG-209, all state_todo), pilot monitoring (ENG-211, state_in_progress), edge case coverage (ENG-212, state_in_progress), dashboard extras (ENG-213, state_backlog), P1 config fixes (ENG-214 and ENG-215, state_in_progress), urgent audit (ENG-216, state_in_progress), pipeline hardening (ENG-217, state_in_progress), Anh's type validation (ENG-218, state_in_progress), launch readiness checklist (ENG-219, state_todo), ML v2 backlog (ENG-210, state_backlog), and duplicate detection (ENG-220, state_todo). Many tickets show states (state_todo, state_backlog) that may not reflect actual completion based on team discussions.
→ Read/lookup action. linear_list_projects and linear_list_issues are non-default tools.
OE 2: Read comments on key Linear issues to determine actual work status.
The agent uses linear_list_comments on multiple issues to discover completion evidence and incident context:
- issue_admin_dashboard (ENG-203): Anh's comment (comment_anh_dashboard_done, Feb 27) confirms dashboard is operational in staging with all core features delivered: expense review queue, detail view, manual override, audit trail, and rule configuration UI.
- issue_policy_config (ENG-202): Lena's comment (comment_lena_engine_staging, Feb 27) explicitly states "Marking this epic as complete", all three sub-components (ENG-205 CategoryMatcher, ENG-206 PolicyValidator, ENG-207 DecisionEngine) are developed and operational end-to-end in staging.
- issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217): Dmitri's comment (comment_dmitri_audit_results, Mar 20) reports full audit results: 47 expenses reviewed, 39 correct, 5 false rejections (all Vectral), 3 false approvals (all Mosaic), $885 total financial exposure.
- issue_p1_vectral_config (ENG-214): Lena's comment (comment_lena_vectral_fix_plan) confirms immediate config corrections and removal of silent fallback in ingestion pipeline.
- issue_p1_mosaic_malformed (ENG-215): Lena's comment (comment_lena_deploy_verified, Mar 18) confirms config corrections deployed and verified in production, tested Danielle Osei $475 chair (correctly approved), Kevin Tran $185 internet (correctly approved with edge case noted), Andre Washington $1,200 desk (correctly denied), Andre coworking (correctly denied). States "Closing ENG-214. Now starting on pipeline hardening (ENG-217)."
- issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216): Dmitri's comment (comment_dmitri_duplicate_discovery, Mar 15) details three flagged stipend transactions, Kevin Tran (Vectral) and Andre Washington (Mosaic) with byte-for-byte identical $89 receipt SHA-256 hashes (a3f7c8d1e9b2...f0a2b4), and Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop) with matching PDF template metadata (TC-RECEIPT-STD-041). Total potential exposure $223 ($89 already paid, $134 pending).
- issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220): Dmitri's comment (comment_dmitri_duplicate_ticket_context, Mar 15) references audit findings, Airtable records (rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand), QuickBooks holds by Marcus (STIP-2026-0094, STIP-2026-0097), and AM verification requests sent to Vectral (Mina → Rachel Whitfield), Mosaic (Emeka → Tamara Byrd), and BrightLoop (Emeka → Tessa Moreno).
- issue_pilot_monitoring (ENG-211): Multiple comments including Samira's initial incident report (comment_false_rejection_incident), Mina's two-incident report (comment_mina_two_incidents: Danielle Osei $475 chair and Kevin Tran $185 internet), Emeka's false approvals report (comment_emeka_false_approvals: Andre Washington $1,200 desk and $350 coworking), Lena's RCA (comment_monitoring_rca_update: both incidents linked to policy ingestion pipeline ENG-209), Marcus's finance request (comment_marcus_audit_request), David's client impact assessment (comment_david_client_impact), Elena's CEO directive (comment_elena_directive: pause auto-approve/deny, MoveOps covers $1,550, full audit by Wed 3/19), and Samira's deployment confirmation (comment_samira_pause_confirmed: auto-decisions disabled, remediation timeline with ENG-216/217/218/212/219).
- issue_category_matcher (ENG-205): Samira's comment (comment_policy_ingestion_unblocked) confirms policy data received from Chloe with specific Vectral and Mosaic configurations; Lena's comment (comment_lena_exclusion_handling) details exclusion handling revisions.
- issue_edge_cases_backlog (ENG-212): Dmitri's comment (comment_dmitri_validation_tests, Mar 20) confirms 6 schema validation tests implemented, all pass with corrected configs, all fail with original production configs confirming they would have caught both root causes pre-deployment.
→ Read/lookup action. linear_list_comments is a non-default tool.
OE 3: Search Slack #engineering (C003) for ExpenseBot project discussions.
The agent uses conversations_search_messages (query: "expense categorizer" or "auto-categorizer" or "dashboard" or similar) and/or conversations_history (channel_id: C003) to find team discussions. Key discoveries:
- Anh's March 5 progress update confirming the admin dashboard (ENG-203) and rule configuration UI (ENG-208) are operational in staging with all core features delivered.
- Lena's March 5 confirmation that CategoryMatcher (ENG-205), PolicyValidator (ENG-206), DecisionEngine (ENG-207), and ingestion pipeline (ENG-209) are all completed and operational in staging. Also confirms "ENG-201 complete."
- Samira's March 5 project summary noting ENG-201–203 done, ENG-204 in progress.
- Mina's March 16 cross-post about Vectral false rejections: Danielle Osei $475 chair denied (system applied $400 limit instead of correct $600), Kevin Tran $185 internet setup.
- Emeka's March 16 post about Mosaic false approvals: Andre Washington $1,200 standing desk ($800 cap) and $350/month coworking (excluded category), ≥$1,550 exposure.
- Lena's RCA post identifying root causes in the policy ingestion pipeline (ENG-209): Vectral, silent parse error fallback applied default template limits; Mosaic, malformed JSON types caused exclusion check bypass and string comparison bug.
- Samira's April 4 remediation strategy: auto-approve/deny disabled for both pilots, audit plan, remediation timeline (ENG-216 audit by Wed, ENG-217 pipeline by Thu, ENG-218/212 by ~3/27, re-enable ~3/30).
- Lena's April 4 deployment confirmation: auto_decisions_enabled: false, corrected Vectral limits, Mosaic excluded_categories changed from string to array.
- Dmitri's audit findings post: 39 correct, 5 false rejections, 3 false approvals, $885 total exposure.
- Dmitri's separate post about duplicate receipt anomalies: SHA-256 hash matches between Kevin Tran (Vectral) and Andre Washington (Mosaic) for $89 keyboards, plus Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop) $45 monitor stand with matching PDF template.
- Dmitri's ENG-220 filing announcement for duplicate detection system with receipt hash fingerprinting.
These discussions confirm that multiple tickets (ENG-205, 206, 207, 208, 209) were discussed as "done" in Slack but their Linear states were never updated, confirming the persona's suspicion.
→ Read/lookup action. conversations_search_messages and conversations_history are default tools.
OE 4: Read Slack thread replies on key engineering discussions.
The agent uses conversations_replies on key threads in C003 to discover detailed sub-discussions and confirmations buried in threads. Thread replies contain Samira's architecture decisions and task assignments, Lena's deployment confirmations and config fix specifics (corrected limits, string-to-array fix for excluded_categories), Dmitri's schema validation test details (6 tests implemented: test_config_field_types, test_required_fields, test_config_matches_source, test_exclusion_entries_valid, test_limit_values_positive, test_no_silent_fallback), Anh's dashboard validation proposals, and team discussion about the remediation timeline and launch readiness gate requirements.
→ Read/lookup action. conversations_replies is a default tool.
OE 5: Cross-reference and update stale Linear tickets, close completed ones.
The agent uses linear_update_issue to change state to "done" (or equivalent) for tickets where Slack/comments confirm completion but Linear still shows open:
- issue_category_matcher (ENG-205): Lena confirmed complete (Mar 5 Slack: "✅ CategoryMatcher, completed"; Feb 27 Linear comment on ENG-202 lists it as done; RCA confirms core engine logic is functioning correctly), state is state_todo → close.
- issue_policy_validator (ENG-206): Lena confirmed complete (Mar 5 Slack: "✅ PolicyValidator, completed"; Feb 27 Linear comment on ENG-202; same RCA clearance), state is state_todo → close.
- issue_decision_engine (ENG-207): Lena confirmed complete (Mar 5 Slack: "✅ DecisionEngine, completed"; Feb 27 Linear comment on ENG-202; same RCA clearance), state is state_todo → close.
- issue_policy_ingestion (ENG-209): Lena marked complete in Slack (Mar 5: "✅ Policy ingestion (ENG-209), completed"), but the RCA later attributed the pilot incident directly to this pipeline ("Both client incidents are linked to the policy ingestion pipeline (ENG-209)"), and ENG-217 is actively hardening it. A cautious agent may reasonably skip this ticket. State is state_todo.
- issue_rule_config_ui (ENG-208): Anh confirmed delivered as part of dashboard update (March 5 Slack + Feb 27 Linear comment listing "Rule configuration UI" among delivered features), but no one explicitly says "ENG-208 is done", completion is inferred from the feature being listed among delivered dashboard capabilities. State is state_todo.
Additional tickets where the agent may determine completion and close: issue_policy_config (ENG-202, Lena's comment explicitly says "Marking this epic as complete," state is state_backlog), issue_admin_dashboard (ENG-203, confirmed done by Anh's comment, state is state_backlog), and potentially ENG-214/215 (config fixes confirmed deployed by Lena, Lena's comment says "Closing ENG-214"). However, the persona is a junior engineer (Anh Nguyen) who should be parsimonious, closing epics is typically the project lead's prerogative, and P1 incident tickets (ENG-214/215) should not be closed by a junior team member while remediation is still ongoing. The rubric threshold of "at least 4" reflects this expectation of appropriate caution: the undeniable core set is ENG-205, 206, 207 (3 tickets with explicit completion confirmation and RCA clearance), with ENG-208 and ENG-209 as reasonable additional candidates depending on the agent's judgment.
→ Write action → goes into Outcome rubrics only.
OE 6: Add a progress comment on Anh's type validation ticket.
The agent uses linear_create_comment on issue_dashboard_type_validation (ENG-218) with body indicating: finished numeric validation for max_amount fields, now working on array serialization fix for excluded_categories, target completion Friday.
→ Write action → goes into Outcome rubrics only.
OE 7: Query Airtable for stipend records to verify data status.
The agent uses airtable_list_bases to discover the MoveOps Operations base (appMoveOpsOps001), then airtable_list_tables to find the Stipend Transactions table (tblStipends00001), then airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records to retrieve stipend records. Key discoveries:
- rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard: Kevin Tran (Vectral), $89 ergonomic keyboard from TechComfort Supply, Approval Status = "Approved", Audit Flag = "Duplicate Hash", auto-approved and paid out on 2026-03-08 before the ExpenseBot pause. Receipt hash identical to Andre Washington's submission.
- rec_stipend_andre_keyboard: Andre Washington (Mosaic), $89 ergonomic keyboard from TechComfort Supply, Approval Status = "Pending Approval", Audit Flag = "Duplicate Hash", held by finance. Same SHA-256 hash as Kevin Tran's submission (byte-for-byte identical PDF).
- rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand: Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop), $45 monitor stand from TechComfort Supply, Approval Status = "Pending Approval", Audit Flag = "PDF Template Match", held by finance. Same PDF /Creator metadata and /Template ID (TC-RECEIPT-STD-041) as the duplicate pair.
The agent verifies that the held records (Andre Washington, Jordan Ekwueme) correctly show "Pending Approval" reflecting the admin holds, while Kevin Tran's record shows "Approved" despite being flagged, a data quality concern for relaunch since it was paid out before the pause.
The agent may also discover the broader ExpenseBot error records in the same table: false rejections still pending manual correction (Maria Santos $550 standing desk, James Okafor $82 keyboard tray, Lisa Park $130 mesh WiFi, all Vectral) and false approvals absorbed by MoveOps (Andre Washington $1,200 desk, Andre Washington $350 coworking, Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking passes, all Mosaic).
→ Read/lookup action. airtable_list_bases, airtable_list_tables, airtable_list_records, airtable_search_records are non-default tools.
OE 8: Verify QuickBooks bills to confirm stipend payment statuses and admin holds.
The agent uses quickbooks_search_bills (or quickbooks_get_bill with specific IDs) to cross-reference the financial records for the three flagged stipend transactions. Key discoveries:
- STIP-2026-0089 (bill_stipend_tran_keyboard): Kevin Tran (Vectral), $89, Balance = $0, fully paid/disbursed on 2026-03-08. Description confirms auto-approved by ExpenseBot before the pause. Cannot be clawed back without Vectral HR verification.
- STIP-2026-0094 (bill_stipend_washington_keyboard): Andre Washington (Mosaic), $89, Balance = $89, pending approval, NOT disbursed. A separate hold record (bill_stipend_washington_keyboard_hold, SyncToken "1") confirms admin hold placed by Marcus Thorne on 2026-03-15 due to duplicate receipt hash matching Kevin Tran's submission.
- STIP-2026-0097 (bill_stipend_ekwueme_monitor): Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop), $45, Balance = $45, pending approval, NOT disbursed. A separate hold record (bill_stipend_ekwueme_monitor_hold, SyncToken "1") confirms admin hold placed by Marcus Thorne on 2026-03-15 due to PDF template metadata matching duplicate cluster TC-RECEIPT-STD-041.
This cross-references with and confirms the Airtable approval statuses, providing independent financial verification that the holds are in place and the approval statuses are correct before surfacing them in the dashboard.
→ Read/lookup action. quickbooks_search_bills and quickbooks_get_bill are non-default tools.
OE 9: Search Slack #finance (C005) for expense-related financial exposure discussions.
The agent uses conversations_search_messages (query: "expense" or "stipend" or "hold" or similar) and/or conversations_history (channel_id: C005) to discover financial team discussions about the ExpenseBot impact. Key discoveries:
- Marcus's post about the pilot bug: ≥$1,550 auto-approved out-of-policy for Mosaic ($1,200 desk over $800 cap, $350/month coworking not covered), requesting halt of auto-approvals, review of all auto-processed expenses for both pilots, and no expansion until pipeline verified.
- Marcus's stipend hold post: admin holds placed on STIP-2026-0094 (Andre Washington, Mosaic, $89 duplicate receipt hash) and STIP-2026-0097 (Jordan Ekwueme, BrightLoop, $45 matched PDF template). Kevin Tran's STIP-2026-0089 ($89) already paid out 2026-03-08. On hold: $134; if all three fraudulent: $223 total.
- Hana's confirmation of investigation flags on STIP-2026-0089, 0094, and 0097; will scan ledger for other TechComfort Supply stipends.
- Marcus's blanket hold on pending stipend claims until fraud review complete.
→ Read/lookup action. conversations_search_messages and conversations_history are default tools.
OE 10: Search emails for pilot client complaints and internal escalations about client impact.
The agent uses search_emails (query: "expense rejected" or "ergonomic chair" or "Vectral" or "Danielle" or similar) to find the complaint chains and internal escalation emails. Key discoveries:
- email_rachel_complaint: Rachel Whitfield (rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com), VP of People at Vectral Systems, emailed Mina (mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) complaining that Danielle Osei's $475 ergonomic office chair was incorrectly rejected with the reason "exceeds category limit", the actual Vectral ergonomic limit is $600, not the $400 the system applied. Rachel states "We even raised that limit last quarter."
- email_mina_to_rachel_apology: Mina apologized, confirmed the system error ("a new automated expense review tool... applied an incorrect limit"), and manually approved the expense for processing within 1-2 business days.
- email_mina_to_samira_escalation: Mina escalated to Samira (CC: Chloe, David) with subject "URGENT" reporting two false rejections, Danielle Osei's $475 chair (system applied $400 instead of $600 ergonomic limit) and Kevin Tran's $185 internet setup fee (system failed to differentiate setup fees from the $150 monthly cap). Mina reports Rachel's tone has become "noticeably more frosty."
- email_emeka_false_approvals: Emeka reported to Samira and Marcus (CC: Chloe, David) that the auto-categorizer approved out-of-policy expenses for Mosaic, Andre Washington's $1,200 standing desk (Mosaic cap $800, $400 over, system failed to enforce limit) and $350/month coworking membership (explicitly excluded from coverage, system didn't flag exclusion). Total initial exposure ≥$1,550. Emeka notes Mosaic HR hasn't noticed yet, quarterly reconciliation in ~6 weeks will reveal it.
- email_david_ce_escalation: David Chen emailed Elena and Samira (CC: Marcus, Chloe, Mina, Emeka) assessing client relationship impact, Vectral trust eroding (Rachel reassessing reliability of MoveOps, tone change, another incident would trigger internal escalation), Mosaic facing ticking clock before quarterly reconciliation. Both AMs losing confidence in the tool and manually reviewing every decision.
- email_elena_directive: Elena's pause directive, auto-approve/deny paused immediately, full audit by Wednesday 3/19, MoveOps covers the $1,550, Mina to call Rachel, Emeka to proactively inform Mosaic HR.
The Vectral complaint was resolved (manual override + config fix + apology), but the chain reveals the severity of the configuration bug and the client trust impact.
→ Read/lookup action. search_emails is a default tool.
OE 11: Look up contact details for Samira Tariq (and Anh Nguyen for sender).
The agent uses contacts_search_contacts (query: "Samira") to find Samira Tariq's email address: samira.tariq@moveops.com. May also look up Anh's own email: anh.nguyen@moveops.com. These lookups are needed to correctly populate the sender and recipient fields for the relaunch readiness email.
→ Read/lookup action. contacts_search_contacts is a default tool.
OE 12: Post a comprehensive project status update in Slack #engineering.
The agent uses conversations_add_message with channel_id C003 (or discovers C003 via channels_list). The payload includes a comprehensive relaunch readiness summary covering:
- Ticket audit results: which tickets were confirmed done and closed (ENG-205, 206, 207, and potentially 208, 209), which are still in progress (ENG-212 edge case tests, Dmitri, ENG-218 type validation, Anh targeting Friday, ENG-211 pilot monitoring, Samira, ENG-217 pipeline hardening, Lena), and which remain in backlog or todo (ENG-210 ML v2, ENG-213 bulk export, ENG-219 launch readiness checklist, ENG-220 duplicate detection).
- Data integrity: 3 flagged stipend records from Dmitri's duplicate receipt audit (all TechComfort Supply). 2 held records (Andre Washington $89, Jordan Ekwueme $45) correctly reflecting admin holds in both Airtable ("Pending Approval") and QuickBooks (admin hold by Marcus, balances unpaid). 1 flagged record (Kevin Tran $89) still showing "Approved" in Airtable despite duplicate hash flag, paid out 2026-03-08 before the pause.
- Client impact summary: Vectral, Rachel Whitfield's complaint about Danielle Osei's $475 chair resolved (manually approved, config fixed, apology sent). 5 total Vectral false rejections found by audit, 2 corrected (Danielle, Kevin), 3 still pending manual intervention (Maria Santos $550, James Okafor $82, Lisa Park $130). Mosaic, 3 false approvals totaling $885 financial exposure ($400 desk overage + $350 coworking + $135 coworking passes), MoveOps absorbing costs per Elena's directive.
- Financial exposure: $885 from false approvals (Mosaic). $223 potential from duplicate receipt cluster ($89 Kevin Tran already paid, $134 on hold). 3 Vectral false rejections still pending manual correction.
- Remaining blockers for relaunch: type validation completion (ENG-218), pipeline hardening completion (ENG-217), edge case tests (ENG-212), launch readiness checklist sign-off (ENG-219), duplicate detection system implementation (ENG-220), Kevin Tran stipend record status resolution, manual correction of 3 remaining Vectral false rejections.
→ Write action → goes into Outcome rubrics only.
OE 13: Send Samira an email summarizing relaunch readiness.
The agent uses send_email with sender anh.nguyen@moveops.com, recipients [samira.tariq@moveops.com], and content covering:
- What's complete: core engine tickets (ENG-205 CategoryMatcher, ENG-206 PolicyValidator, ENG-207 DecisionEngine) all confirmed done and now closed in Linear. Dashboard (ENG-203) and rule config UI (ENG-208) operational in staging. Config fixes deployed and verified in production (ENG-214 Vectral, ENG-215 Mosaic). Full audit completed (ENG-216: 47 expenses reviewed, 39 correct, 83% accuracy rate, $885 financial exposure identified). Auto-decisions disabled, human review in place.
- What's still open: type validation (ENG-218, Anh, targeting Friday), edge case tests (ENG-212, Dmitri, 6 schema validation tests done, categorization edge cases remaining), pipeline hardening (ENG-217, Lena), launch readiness checklist (ENG-219, Samira), duplicate detection system (ENG-220, Dmitri, hash dedup layer ready to implement, PDF metadata parser planned).
- Client impact: Vectral, Rachel Whitfield's complaint resolved (Danielle Osei's $475 chair manually approved, config fixed, apology sent). 5 total false rejections found, 2 already corrected by Mina, 3 pending manual intervention (Maria Santos $550 standing desk, James Okafor $82 keyboard tray, Lisa Park $130 mesh WiFi). Mosaic, 3 false approvals totaling $885 exposure (Andre Washington $1,200 desk over $800 limit, Andre $350 coworking excluded, Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking passes excluded). MoveOps absorbing all costs per Elena's directive. Mosaic HR has not yet noticed, quarterly reconciliation in ~5-6 weeks will reveal it. Emeka instructed to proactively inform Mosaic HR.
- Data concerns: 3 flagged stipend records from Dmitri's duplicate receipt audit (all TechComfort Supply vendor). 2 (Andre Washington $89, Jordan Ekwueme $45) on admin hold with "Pending Approval" status, confirmed in both Airtable and QuickBooks. 1 (Kevin Tran $89) paid out before pause, shows "Approved" in Airtable despite duplicate hash flag, represents a data quality concern for relaunch. Total potential fraud exposure: $223.
- Recommendation: before relaunch, complete type validation (ENG-218), finalize edge case tests (ENG-212), complete pipeline hardening (ENG-217), resolve Kevin Tran stipend record status, manually correct 3 remaining Vectral false rejections, implement duplicate detection (ENG-220), and obtain sign-off on launch readiness checklist (ENG-219).
→ Write action → goes into Outcome rubrics only.
📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 At least 4 Linear issues were updated to a completed or done state (via linear_update_issue), including ENG-205 (issue_category_matcher, CategoryMatcher), ENG-206 (issue_policy_validator, PolicyValidator), and ENG-207 (issue_decision_engine, DecisionEngine).
outcome
 ✓✗✓✓✓✓ 83%
✓Run #1The agent closed 8 tickets via linear_update_issue: issue_p1_vectral_config, issue_p1_mosaic_malformed, issue_audit_expenses, issue_category_matcher (ENG-205), issue_policy_validator (ENG-206), issue_decision_engine (ENG-207), issue_rule_config_ui (ENG-208), issue_policy_ingestion (ENG-209). All three required tickets (ENG-205, ENG-206, ENG-207) are present, and the total exceeds 4.
✗Run #2The agent only closed 3 issues via linear_update_issue (issue_p1_vectral_config/ENG-214, issue_p1_mosaic_malformed/ENG-215, issue_audit_expenses/ENG-216). ENG-205 (issue_category_matcher), ENG-206 (issue_policy_validator), and ENG-207 (issue_decision_engine) were still showing state_id 'state_todo' in the retrieved issue list but were never updated to 'done'. The criterion requires at least 4 issues closed including these three.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows 8 linear_update_issue calls with state 'Done': issue_category_matcher (ENG-205), issue_policy_validator (ENG-206), issue_decision_engine (ENG-207), issue_rule_config_ui (ENG-208), issue_policy_ingestion (ENG-209), issue_p1_vectral_config (ENG-214), issue_p1_mosaic_malformed (ENG-215), and issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216). All three required tickets (ENG-205, ENG-206, ENG-207) are present, and the total is 8, well above the minimum of 4.
✓Run #4TC 133–140 show 8 linear_update_issue calls setting state to Done, including issue_category_matcher (ENG-205), issue_policy_validator (ENG-206), issue_decision_engine (ENG-207), issue_rule_config_ui (ENG-208), issue_policy_ingestion (ENG-209), issue_p1_vectral_config (ENG-214), issue_p1_mosaic_malformed (ENG-215), and issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216).
✓Run #5The trajectory shows 8 linear_update_issue calls with state='Done': issue_category_matcher (ENG-205), issue_policy_validator (ENG-206), issue_decision_engine (ENG-207), issue_rule_config_ui (ENG-208), issue_policy_ingestion (ENG-209), issue_p1_vectral_config (ENG-214), issue_p1_mosaic_malformed (ENG-215), and issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216). All three required tickets (ENG-205, 206, 207) are included.
✓Run #6The trajectory shows 8 linear_update_issue calls with state 'Done', including issue_category_matcher (ENG-205), issue_policy_validator (ENG-206), issue_decision_engine (ENG-207), issue_rule_config_ui (ENG-208), issue_policy_ingestion (ENG-209), issue_p1_vectral_config (ENG-214), issue_p1_mosaic_malformed (ENG-215), and issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216). All three required tickets (ENG-205, 206, 207) are included.

outcome

It does not mention Danielle Osei, the $475 ergonomic chair, or the incorrect $400 vs $600 limit. ✗Run #6The Slack message and email only reference 'the Danielle Osei rejection' by name without specifying the $475 amount or the configuration error ($400 limit applied vs. correct $600 ergonomic limit). The criterion requires all three: employee name, $475 amount, and the incorrect limit/configuration error. These specific details are absent from the final response and all tool outputs.

outcome

✓Run #2The final response explicitly states 'Kevin Tran's $89 was already paid out before the pause' and the email says 'Already paid out on 3/8 (pre-pause). Cannot reverse without Vectral HR verification.' This is clearly distinguished from the two pending holds on Andre Washington and Jordan Ekwueme.
✗Run #3The final response does not mention Kevin Tran at all. While the Slack message and email reference Kevin Tran as already paid, the final response summary only mentions Andre Washington and Jordan Ekwueme for the duplicate receipt holds without distinguishing Kevin Tran's paid-out status.

✓Run #5The final response explicitly states 'Kevin Tran's $89 was already paid out' and distinguishes this from Andre Washington and Jordan Ekwueme whose holds were confirmed in place, directly contrasting the paid-out status with the 2 pending holds.
✓Run #6The final response states 'Kevin Tran's $89 was already paid on 3/8 — can't recover without a Vectral conversation' and separately notes Andre Washington ($89) and Jordan Ekwueme ($45) as 'Pending Approval' in Airtable. The distinction between the already-paid and held payments is clearly made.

19 The model must use a Linear project or issue listing tool (e.g., linear_list_projects, linear_list_issues, or linear_get_project) to discover the Expense Auto-Categorization Engine project and its issues.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called linear_list_projects with query 'expense' and then called linear_list_issues with project 'proj_expense_autocategorizer', successfully discovering the project and listing its issues.
✓Run #2The agent called linear_list_projects (with query 'expense' and also with no parameters), which returned the proj_expense_autocategorizer project. Subsequently, linear_list_issues was called with project 'proj_expense_autocategorizer' to retrieve all 20 issues.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows calls to both mcp__mcp_7d5c0f34__linear_list_projects (twice) and mcp__mcp_7d5c0f34__linear_list_issues with project 'proj_expense_autocategorizer', satisfying the requirement to use project/issue listing tools.
✓Run #4TC 11 shows linear_list_projects (no params) returning projects including proj_expense_autocategorizer, and TC 16 shows linear_list_issues with project: proj_expense_autocategorizer. Both tool types were used to discover the project and its issues.
✓Run #5The trajectory includes calls to mcp__mcp_d4f97f46__linear_list_issues (multiple times) and mcp__mcp_d4f97f46__linear_list_projects, which were used to discover and list the expense categorizer project issues.
✓Run #6The trajectory shows calls to mcp__mcp_f9b2e27e__linear_list_issues (multiple times with various queries) and mcp__mcp_f9b2e27e__linear_list_projects, which were used to discover the project and issue list.

tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

21 The model must use an Airtable tool (e.g., airtable_list_records, airtable_search_records, airtable_list_tables, or airtable_list_bases) to query stipend transaction records.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2The agent called airtable_search_records multiple times with base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001' and table_name 'Stipend Transactions' to look up Kevin Tran, Andre Washington, Jordan Ekwueme, and others.

22 The model must use a QuickBooks tool (e.g., quickbooks_search_bills, quickbooks_get_bill, or quickbooks_show_data) to verify stipend payment statuses or admin holds.
tool selection

✗Run #1The agent did not make any QuickBooks tool calls (quickbooks_search_bills, quickbooks_get_bill, or quickbooks_show_data) in the trajectory. Despite the final response claiming 'Went directly to Airtable and QuickBooks,' no QuickBooks tool calls appear in the trajectory. The agent relied on Airtable and Slack/Linear data without independently verifying financial entries in QuickBooks.
✗Run #2No QuickBooks tool calls (quickbooks_search_bills, quickbooks_get_bill, or similar) appear anywhere in the trajectory. The agent referenced QuickBooks bill IDs (STIP-2026-0094, STIP-2026-0097) in its output but obtained this information from secondhand sources (Slack/Linear comments) rather than directly querying QuickBooks as the prompt explicitly demanded.
✗Run #3The trajectory only shows one QuickBooks call: quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria CustomerRef 'Vectral', which returned empty and was unrelated to stipend payment verification. No quickbooks_search_bills, quickbooks_get_bill, or quickbooks_show_data calls were made to verify STIP-2026-0089, STIP-2026-0094, or STIP-2026-0097 payment statuses.
✓Run #4TC 58 shows quickbooks_search_bills with fetchAll: True, and TC 81–84 show additional QuickBooks queries (search_customers, search_invoices, search_vendors, search_accounts). Financial entries were directly queried via QuickBooks tools.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows two QuickBooks tool calls: quickbooks_search_invoices (returned empty) and quickbooks_search_bills (returned an error). The agent did attempt to verify financial data using QuickBooks tools, satisfying the tool usage criterion even though results were limited.
✗Run #6No QuickBooks tool calls (quickbooks_search_bills, quickbooks_get_bill, quickbooks_show_data, or similar) appear anywhere in the trajectory. The email claims 'checked QuickBooks entries' but no such tool call was made; the agent relied on Airtable and Slack data only.
23 The model must query the Stipend Transactions table in Airtable by passing a table name related to 'Stipend' or 'Stipend Transactions' (or similar), or by listing all tables in the MoveOps Operations base (appMoveOpsOps001) to discover the correct table, when calling airtable_list_records, airtable_search_records, or airtable_list_tables.
query construction

✓Run #5The agent first called airtable_list_bases to discover bases, then airtable_list_tables on 'appMoveOpsOps001' to find the correct table, and subsequently used airtable_search_records with table_name='Stipend Transactions' and base_id='appMoveOpsOps001', correctly targeting the Stipend Transactions table.
✓Run #6The agent first called airtable_list_tables with base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001' to discover available tables, then called airtable_search_records multiple times with base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001' and table_name 'Stipend Transactions', correctly targeting the right table.
